text.title: Conversion Definition and Value Assignment
text.summary: Macro vs micro conversion taxonomy, value assignment methods, and event name mapping across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn.

rule.statement: Always track both macro conversions (primary business goal) and micro conversions (funnel steps) — micro conversions provide optimization signal when macro volume is too low for smart bidding.
rule.rationale: Smart bidding requires 30-50 conversions per week to function; new campaigns rarely achieve this on macro events alone. Micro conversions (add-to-cart, pricing page view) fill the signal gap during ramp-up.

list.item: Macro — Purchase, Trial start, Lead form submit, Account registration
Micro — Add to cart, Begin checkout, Pricing page view, Content download
Dynamic value — Purchase = actual transaction amount (recommended)
Estimated LTV value — Trial = $100 (based on historical LTV/conversion rate)

rule.statement: Pass dynamic purchase values whenever possible — static values prevent Target ROAS bidding from optimizing for high-value orders over low-value ones.
rule.rationale: Static values make all conversions look equal to the algorithm; with dynamic values, bidding can allocate more spend toward user segments that produce larger orders.

list.item: Add to cart: Meta=AddToCart, Google=add_to_cart, LinkedIn=N/A
